commit.message:
fix: character select uses direct key events (v2) (#118)

* fix: character select uses direct key events, bypasses action map

FUNDAMENTALLY different approach from PR #116 (which failed):
- PR #116 added ui_accept/ui_left/ui_right action references but never
  added those actions to project.godot. Godot 4 built-in defaults
  apparently don't work reliably in exported builds.

This fix bypasses the input action system entirely for character select:
- character_select.gd now uses _unhandled_input() with direct
  InputEventKey keycode matching (KEY_LEFT, KEY_RIGHT, KEY_ENTER, etc.)
- No dependency on ui_* or p1_* actions — keys always work
- Enter/Space/KP Enter to confirm, Left/Right/A/D to navigate,
  Escape to go back
- Removed _process() polling approach entirely

Additional fixes:
- Added explicit ui_accept/ui_cancel/ui_left/ui_right/ui_up/ui_down
  actions to project.godot so main menu keyboard navigation (Button
  focus system) also works with Enter/Space/Arrows/Escape
- Removed VS Player button from main menu (singleplayer only for now)
- Fixed main menu focus neighbor chain after button removal

Flow: Main Menu (VS CPU only) → CharacterSelect (arrows to pick,
Enter to confirm, Esc to go back) → Fight scene

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: update Lando history with charselect v2 fix

commit.message:
fix: rate limit not detected when SDK sends result error after rate_limit_event

Two bugs prevented rate limit state from being set, causing the loop to
fall through to idle threshold and enter SLEEPING instead of showing the
rate limit banner.

Bug 1 (AgentSdkLauncher): When the SDK emits a rate_limit_event (rejected)
and then exits with a result error message, errorMessage was overwritten by
the result error data, losing the rate limit text. The catch-block override
at line 279 only fired on exceptions, not on clean process exits. Fix:
check rateLimitText after setting errorMessage from the result error and
prefer it so parseRateLimitReset can detect it downstream.

Bug 2 (rateLimitParser): When resetsAt is absent from the rate_limit_event,
buildRateLimitText returns 'You've hit your limit · rate limited' (no time
info). parseRateLimitReset couldn't match this and returned null. Fix: add
a Pattern 3 that matches 'You've hit your limit' and returns a 1-hour
default backoff.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Build XRT from source instead of PPA

Replaces the PPA-based XRT install with a full source build from
amd/xdna-driver. This ensures compatibility with newer kernels (6.17+)
and hardware variants like Ryzen AI 7 PRO 360 (NPU4/AIE2P), where
the PPA's XRT 2.20.0 fails.

Three-stage Docker build:
1. xrt-builder: builds XRT base + NPU plugin from source
2. flm-builder: builds FastFlowLM against source-built XRT
3. runtime: minimal image with only runtime libs (440MB)

Also updates README with expanded hardware list and troubleshooting
for XRT version mismatches and NPU4 firmware setup.

Fixes #1

fix: character select accepts Enter/Space and arrow keys (#116)

Character select was only responding to fight controls (U key to confirm,
A/D to navigate), making the screen appear completely stuck to anyone
who didn't know the fight keybindings.

Changes:
- P1 can now confirm with Enter/Space (ui_accept) in addition to U
- P1 can navigate with arrow keys (ui_left/ui_right) in vs_cpu mode

commit.message:
Fix exception handling on Linux: rethrow, class-type exceptions, catch variable binding, and stack unwinding (#865)

* Fix throw; (rethrow) on Linux: remove misaligned sub rsp and extend LSDA coverage to full function

Co-authored-by: GregorGullwi <1397711+GregorGullwi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix class-type exception handling on Linux: proper typeinfo, destructor arg, catch variable binding

Co-authored-by: GregorGullwi <1397711+GregorGullwi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Implement stack unwinding with local destructors (Linux/ELF)

Phase 1: try-block-local variable cleanup
- Add cleanup_vars field to CatchBeginOp to carry {struct_name, var_name}
  pairs captured from the innermost try-block scope
- Add capture_try_cleanup_ state to AstToIr; exitScope() records vars at
  the captured depth before emitting normal destructor calls
- visitTryStatementNode() enables capture around the try body and attaches
  the collected vars to the first CatchBeginOp
- handleCatchBegin() (ELF path) calls emitInlineDestructorCall() for each
  cleanup var after saving RAX/RDX but before selector dispatch, so
  try-block locals are destroyed on the landing pad entry

Phase 2: function-level cleanup landing pad
- Add FunctionCleanupLP opcode and FunctionCleanupLPOp struct
- exitFunctionScope() captures function-scope vars in LIFO order and
  stores them in pending_function_cleanup_vars_ before calling exitScope()
- emitPendingFunctionCleanupLP() emits FunctionCleanupLP after the return
  instruction so the LP is unreachable in normal execution
- handleFunctionCleanupLP() (ELF only): records cleanup_lp_offset, saves
  RAX (_Unwind_Exception*) to a temp frame slot, calls each destructor,
  then loads the saved pointer into RDI and calls _Unwind_Resume
- ElfFileWriter::CleanupBlockInfo added; add_function_exception_info
  accepts an optional cleanup_blocks vector and generates LSDA call-site
  entries (lp=cleanup_lp, action=0) so the personality routine routes
  phase-2 unwinding through the cleanup LP
- IRConverter passes current_function_cleanup_lp_offset_ as a
  CleanupBlockInfo to add_function_exception_info

Tests:
- tests/test_local_dtor_in_try_ret0.cpp  (Phase 1: dtor called in try block)
- tests/test_dtor_propagate_ret0.cpp      (Phase 2: dtors called on propagation)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review feedback

- Tighten Phase 1 test: check g_dtor_calls == 1 (not >=1) to catch
  double-destructor bugs
- Log a warning when emitInlineDestructorCall cannot find a variable
  instead of silently skipping it
- Use size_t for capture_try_cleanup_depth_ to match scope_stack_.size()
  type, removing the signed/unsigned cast

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Implement stack unwinding with local destructors (Linux): Phase 1 + Phase 2 cleanup landing pads

Co-authored-by: GregorGullwi <1397711+GregorGullwi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: re-parse CV-qualifiers after __restrict in pointer-level loops

In `int * __restrict const * p`, CV-qualifiers appearing after
`__restrict` on the same pointer level were not captured in `ptr_cv`
and instead mis-attributed to the base type.  After skipping
`__restrict`, re-parse CV-qualifiers so they are correctly associated
with the pointer level.

Applied to all three affected sites:
- parse_type_and_name()
- parse_declarator()
- consume_pointer_ref_modifiers()

Co-authored-by: devin-ai-integration[bot] <158243242+devin-ai-integration[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* Implemented IdentifierNode refactoring from IdentifierResolutionPlan.md #857 (#861)

* Fix C++20 point-of-declaration: sizeof(x) in own initializer

Per [basic.scope.pdecl], a variable name is visible from the point
immediately after its declarator, before the initializer. This means
sizeof(x) in 'int x = sizeof(x)' is well-formed and evaluates to 4.

Root cause: three-part failure chain:
1. Parser pre-insertion missing: x was not in gSymbolTable when the
   initializer was parsed, so sizeof(x) was mis-parsed as sizeof of an
   unknown UserDefined type with size 0.
2. sizeof disambiguation: the UserDefined/size-0 check did not look up
   the symbol table to detect known variables.
3. Codegen pre-registration missing: even after fixing the parser to
   produce a from_expression sizeof node, the codegen's local symbol_table
   did not contain x when generateSizeofIr evaluated the initializer.

Changes:
- src/SymbolTable.h: add replace_variable() for updating a pre-inserted
  stub with its final node.
- src/Parser_Statements.cpp: in parse_variable_declaration(), pre-insert
  a stub VariableDeclarationNode (no init) for block-scope variables
  before parsing the initializer; replace with the real node after.
- src/Parser_Expr_PrimaryUnary.cpp: sizeof disambiguation now falls
  through to expression parsing when the identifier resolves to a
  VariableDeclarationNode in the symbol table.
- src/CodeGen_Stmt_Decl.cpp: register the variable in the codegen's
  local symbol_table before evaluating the initializer expression so
  that lookupDeclaration() succeeds during sizeof evaluation.
- tests/test_initializer_point_of_declaration_ret4.cpp: regression test.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Identifier resolution Phase 1A: IdentifierBinding enum, extend IdentifierNode, createBoundIdentifier()

- Add IdentifierBinding enum (12 variants: Unresolved/Local/Global/StaticLocal/
  StaticMember/NonStaticMember/CapturedByValue/Ref/This/CopyThis/EnumConstant/Function)
  to AstNodeTypes_DeclNodes.h
- Extend IdentifierNode with binding_, resolved_name_ fields and accessors
  (binding(), set_binding(), resolved_name(), set_resolved_name(), is_resolved())
- Add createBoundIdentifier(Token) inline helper to Parser.h that classifies
  Local/Global/Function/EnumConstant via ordinary symbol-table lookup; leaves
  dependent/unknown names Unresolved (safe fallback for codegen)
- Add get_scope_type_of_symbol() to SymbolTable.h for scope classification
- Update 16 identifier creation sites in Parser_Expr_PrimaryExpr.cpp to call
  createBoundIdentifier() instead of plain IdentifierNode(token)
- Fix typo: rename idenfifier_token -> identifier_token (308 occurrences)
- Add TDD overload-set tests (both pass with current compiler confirming
  using-directive/declaration semantics already correct)

All 1327 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Identifier resolution Phase 1B: use binding() in codegen fast-paths

- generateIdentifierIr(): fast-path for Global binding avoids multi-table lookup
  cascade. Skips fast-path when using-declarations override name resolution
  (e.g., using ::globalValue inside a namespace) to ensure correctness.
- Assignment (=) handler: use binding() == Global to skip detectGlobalOrStaticVar;
  fall through to detectGlobalOrStaticVar for all other bindings (including Local
  which covers static locals with global storage).
- Compound-assignment (+=/-= etc.) handler: same binding-aware approach, plus
  direct global table lookup for type/size info.
- Inc/dec (++/--) handler: use binding() == Global fast-path, fall through
  to detectGlobalOrStaticVar for all other bindings.
- detectGlobalOrStaticVar() retained as fallback for Unresolved/Local bindings

standard, grouped into 9 categories:

  fallback, mangling wrong-type fallbacks
- Lambda: capture-all by odr-use, constexpr capture limits
- Templates: NTTP deduction, silent instantiation fallback

- Exception handling: __cpp_exceptions absent, cross-refs EXCEPTION_HANDLING.md

* Fix integer divide/modulo when divisor is in RAX

Port of FlashCpp2 commit dbe67b66. When the divisor expression
evaluates into RAX (e.g. a function return value), the subsequent
mov RAX, <dividend> overwrites it before IDIV, producing wrong
results (effectively RAX/RAX = 1).

Add three helpers to IRConverter_Conv_Arithmetic.h:
- reserveDivisionFixedRegisters(): extracted flush+lock of RAX/RDX
- allocateDivisionScratchRegister(): finds a scratch reg from
  RCX/R8-R11 avoiding specified registers

* Fix float conversion XMM reuse under register pressure

Port of FlashCpp2 commit 423edce9. In handleFloatToFloat, source_xmm
and result_xmm are allocated sequentially. Under XMM register pressure
the allocator may return the same register for both, causing
regAlloc.release(source_xmm) to free the result register before it
is stored.

Guard the release: only release source_xmm when it differs from
result_xmm.

conversion with 15 live float values to maximise XMM pressure.

* Fix: only set is_global_or_static when global symbol lookup actually succeeds

The `is_global_or_static = true` was placed outside the `if (sym.has_value())`
block in analyzeAddressExpression, causing it to be set even when the global
symbol table lookup returned nullopt. This resulted in returning
AddressComponents with final_type=Void and final_size_bits=0, which could
cause incorrect memory accesses downstream in ComputeAddress generation.

The old detectGlobalOrStaticVar only set this flag when the lookup succeeded
AND the symbol had valid type information. This restores that safety check.

* Use optional<TemplateDepthGuard> instead of ScopedState + manual ++ for template depth

Replace the mixed ScopedState/manual-increment pattern with the idiomatic
conditional TemplateDepthGuard used everywhere else. Fixes indentation and
improves consistency. No behavioral change.

* Revert simple assignment StaticMember change: isValid() assignment is fine here since gsi has no type/size fields

* Enforce C++20 [temp.res]/9 Phase 1 two-phase name lookup

Non-dependent names used inside a template body must be visible at
template definition time. Using a name declared after the template
definition is now rejected as ill-formed.

- Parser.h: Added phase1_cutoff_line_, phase1_cutoff_file_idx_,
  phase1_violation_token_ fields. createBoundIdentifier() now calls
  checkPhase1 for global-scope FunctionDeclarationNode,
  TemplateFunctionDeclarationNode, DeclarationNode, and
  VariableDeclarationNode.

  sets the cutoff from the saved template body opening-brace token and
  throws CompileError after parse if a violation was detected.
- Parser_Templates_Substitution.cpp: Reverted to promoting
  Unresolved -> concrete (the reparse path handles the real check;
  throwing here caused false positives for 'this', args_0, etc.).
- Parser_Templates_Lazy.cpp: Re-throw CompileError before the two
  catch(std::exception&) blocks that were swallowing it.
- main.cpp: Catch CompileError in the parse phase and exit 1.

- Added test_template_rebind_late_global_fail.cpp
- Removed test_template_rebind_late_global_ret0.cpp (was testing

feat: BMW binary transport router with branch-free jump table dispatch (#1328)

Wire format: 6-byte frames [opcode:14][id:32][reserved:2]
Opcode = (method << 11) | route — 8 methods x 2048 routes = 16384 slots.

BmwBinaryTransport.cs:
- Dense Handler[16384] jump table populated at startup
- BinaryPrimitives + Span for zero-allocation frame decoding
- AggressiveInlining on DecodeFrame, EncodeFrame, GetMethod, GetRoute
- Write methods (POST/PUT/PATCH) carry 24-bit content length prefix
- ProcessAsync loop: read WebSocket frames, decode, dispatch — branch-free
- PopulateFromRoutes wraps existing RouteHandlerDelegates into jump table
- Default 404 handler fills all unregistered slots (no branching needed)

BmwWebSocketHandler.cs:
- HTTP -> WebSocket upgrade handler at GET /bmw/ws
- Auth occurs during HTTP upgrade, BmwContext stored for connection lifetime
- Graceful close + exception handling for disconnects

Also fixes pre-existing RenderPlan build errors:
- Added Compile/Execute forwarding methods on RenderPlan class
- Added IBufferWriter<byte> overloads to TemplatePlanExecutor

39 tests pass covering frame encoding/decoding, payload length,
method ordinals, jump table registration, and edge cases.

Closes #1324

commit.message:
fix: refresh expired Discord CDN image URLs; add frontend fallback

Discord CDN attachment URLs contain time-limited signatures (ex=, is=, hm=
query params) that expire after ~24 hours. Old pins in Cosmos DB have URLs
that are now returning 404.

API fix (primary):
- api/src/shared/discordRefresh.ts: new module that calls Discord's
  POST /api/v10/attachments/refresh-urls endpoint to get fresh signed URLs.
  Batches in groups of 50, caches results for 11 hours in memory, falls back
  to original URL on any error.
- api/src/functions/getPins.ts: calls refreshDiscordUrls() before serializing
  the response, so the frontend always receives a fresh URL.
- infra/modules/functions.bicep: add DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN app setting via Key
  Vault reference (discord-bot-token secret already exists).
- Applied DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN to production immediately via az CLI.

Frontend fallback (safety net):
- frontend/js/map.js: attach an error listener on popupopen to detect 404s.
  On failure, hides the broken img and shows a faded hat-logo placeholder
  with 'Image no longer available' + a direct Discord message link.
- frontend/index.html: add CSS for .popup-img-error and .popup-img-error-icon.
  Uses display:none toggle — no inline event handlers, fully CSP-safe.

commit.message:
docs: add bounded-context AGENTS.md files; fix ArtifactGenerator and CLI logging bugs (#54)

Adds 10 `*_AGENTS.md` files across all host projects, Core sub-contexts,
and test projects (ADR-015 naming). A review pass surfaced two code
bugs, both fixed here.

## Description

**New AGENTS.md files (10)**
- `CORE_AGENTS.md` — two-writer fan-in constraint, `"critical"` tier
caveat, stdin safety for external processes
- `CLI_AGENTS.md` — command contracts, stdout/stderr split, `index` vs
`reindex` distinction
- `MCP_AGENTS.md` — stdout == JSON-RPC (never write there), shared tool
assembly constraint
- `API_AGENTS.md` — `/health` + `/search-results/` contracts, `public
partial class Program` requirement
- `DISCOVERY_AGENTS.md` — case-insensitive discovery (ADR-011),
BoundedContext prefix inference (ADR-015)
- `INDEXING_AGENTS.md` — DeepIndex two-writer sequence diagram, ripgrep
stdin deadlock fix, hybrid two-pass search
- `GENERATORS_AGENTS.md` — manifest as readiness signal,
`agents_md_cache` subdirectory behavior
- `SDK_AGENTS.md` — filesystem-only client, stale-cache behavior,
null-guard contracts
- `UNIT_TESTS_AGENTS.md` — FluentAssertions mandate, fixture
immutability, temp directory isolation
- `INTEGRATION_TESTS_AGENTS.md` — `WebApplicationFactory` setup,
protocol-only test scope

**Code bug fixes**
- `ArtifactGenerator.cs`: ADR-015 prefixed files produce BoundedContext
values containing `/` (e.g.
`smooth.coderepositoryindexmcp.core/indexing`). `Path.Combine(cacheDir,
$"{boundedContext}.json")` then targets a non-existent subdirectory —
`File.WriteAllText` throws `DirectoryNotFoundException`. Fix:
`Directory.CreateDirectory(Path.GetDirectoryName(cacheFilePath)!)`
before each write.
- `CLI/appsettings.json`: Serilog Console sink defaulted to stdout,
corrupting piped output (e.g. `smooth-code-repo-index domain-map | grep
billing`). Fix: `"standardErrorFromLevel": "Verbose"` routes all log
output to stderr.

**Documentation drift fixes (applied across AGENTS.md files)**
- Section order corrected (Architecture Decisions §4 before Key
Behaviors §5) in 7 files
- Missing `Date` field added to all 12 LADRs
- Non-Negotiables promoted from wrong sections for CLI stdout, API
`partial class Program`, and Discovery case-sensitivity
- `INTEGRATION_TESTS_AGENTS.md`: factory uses
`Utilities.ResolveRepoRoot()`, not a hardcoded relative path; test scope
no longer claims tool-call coverage that doesn't exist
- `INDEXING_AGENTS.md`: `DeleteDocsByPath` is a null-conditional no-op;
`DeterminePriority` checks path patterns, not "symbol declarations"

## Type of Change

- [x] Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (existing functionality would change)
- [ ] Refactoring (no functional changes)
- [x] Documentation update
- [ ] Build / CI/CD

## Testing

- [ ] Unit tests added / updated
- [ ] Integration tests added / updated
- [x] Manual testing performed

**Test steps:**

1. `dotnet test` — 244 unit + 4 integration tests, all pass
2. Verify piped CLI output is clean: `smooth-code-repo-index domain-map
2>/dev/null` should contain only domain map content with no Serilog
lines

## Checklist

- [x] I have performed a self-review of my own code
- [x] My changes follow the project's coding standards
- [ ] I have added tests that prove my fix or feature works
- [x] New and existing tests pass locally
- [x] No new compiler warnings introduced
- [x] Documentation updated (if applicable)
- [ ] Dependencies reviewed (if new packages added)

## AI Review Notes

**Focus Areas:**
- `ArtifactGenerator.cs` line 59:
`Path.GetDirectoryName(cacheFilePath)!` — null suppression is safe
because `Path.Combine` always produces a path with a non-null directory
component
- `CLI appsettings.json`: `standardErrorFromLevel: Verbose` +
`restrictedToMinimumLevel: Information` interaction — messages below
`Information` are filtered entirely; `Information`+ go to stderr

**Context:**
- The `ArtifactGenerator` bug only manifests once `*_AGENTS.md` files
with a parent-directory namespace exist (ADR-015). Previously all
BoundedContexts were flat strings.
- `IndexClient.GetAgentsContent(boundedContext)` also uses
`Path.Combine(cacheDir, $"{boundedContext}.json")` — this correctly
resolves to the subdirectory path created by the fixed
`ArtifactGenerator`; no client-side change needed.

**Known Issues / Intentional Patterns:**
- No new targeted unit test for the `ArtifactGenerator` subdirectory
fix; existing `FullTextStoreTests` + integration tests provide indirect
coverage. A dedicated test for nested BoundedContext cache writes is a
worthwhile follow-up.
- AGENTS.md files are documentation only — all functional impact is the
two code fixes.

commit.message:
fix: handle Spotify intl- locale prefix in URL parsing

ParseSpotifyUrl was treating 'intl-pt' as the resource type and 'track'
as the ID for URLs like open.spotify.com/intl-pt/track/ID. Now correctly
skips locale prefixes (intl-*) to extract the actual type and ID.

Also added diagnostic logging to SpotifyService and SoundboardController.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: generator, iterator, proxy conformance fixes (#447)

* fix: generator conformance — @@iterator returns self, return on completed preserves value

- Add Generator.prototype[@@iterator]() that returns the generator itself
  per ECMAScript §27.5.1.2, making generators proper iterables.

- Fix generator_return on completed generators to return {value: VALUE,
  done: true} instead of {value: undefined, done: true}, conforming to
  §27.5.3.4 GeneratorResumeAbrupt step 2a.

- Add tests for @@iterator, non-resumability after return/throw, and
  return-on-completed with/without argument.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix iterator protocol handlers in for-of loop dispatch

- GetIterator: pass 'this' (the iterable) when calling @@iterator method
  so custom iterables that reference 'this' in their Symbol.iterator work
- GetAsyncIterator: same this-binding fix for @@asyncIterator/@@iterator
- IteratorNext: pass 'this' (the iterator) when calling .next() on
  PlainObject iterators so stateful custom iterators work correctly
- CreateArrayFromIterable: add missing String, Generator, and PlainObject
  iterator support so [...generator], [...'hello'], and [...customIterator]
  correctly use the iterator protocol instead of producing empty arrays
- Make dispatch_call_value and dispatch_call_with_this pub(super) so
  dispatch.rs can use them

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(proxy): add missing handler trap extractions and pass target to traps

- build_proxy_handler now accepts target_val and passes it to all
  handler trap closures instead of JsValue::Undefined, conforming to
  ECMAScript 10.5 which requires the target object as the first
  argument to every trap.

- Added extraction of 4 additional handler traps from the JS handler
  object: construct, ownKeys, defineProperty, getOwnPropertyDescriptor.
  Previously only get/set/has/deleteProperty/apply were wired up.

- construct trap converts the returned PlainObject into a JsObject.
- ownKeys trap converts the returned array into Vec<String>.
- getOwnPropertyDescriptor trap parses the returned descriptor object
  into (JsValue, PropertyAttributes).
- defineProperty trap forwards the call to the JS handler function.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
